title: Never give up, never say never
sub_topics: Esmel fellowships (entrepreneurship, sustainability, management, economics, business law)
Renaissance Jurist of the 21st Century resolution
AI-powered Museum for Liberty and Prosperity

stances.claim: Life is a strange interplay of light and shadows — when a door closes, a much larger and better window opens; one must keep trying, never give up.
stances.rhetorical_move: aphoristic life-philosophy as opening frame, illustrated by personal biography

stances.claim: FLP's core mission is to translate the philosophy of liberty and prosperity from embedded 'legal lore' into practical reality outside the intellectual realm of lawyers, reaching into the world of hard knocks and grinding poverty.
stances.rhetorical_move: mission framing + autobiographical narrative of post-retirement inspiration ('Ageless Passion' musicale)

stances.claim: The best way to nurture prosperity and eliminate extreme poverty is to unleash the entrepreneurial ingenuity of the people through a sustainable Prosperity Fund that nurtures, grows, manages, and matures MSMEs.
Civic philanthropy is not merely a duty but obedience to Christ's command: 'Whatever you do for the least of my brethren, you do unto me.' (Matthew 25:40)
stances.rhetorical_move: biblical citation as moral foundation for charitable action

signature_phrases: Life is a strange interplay of light and shadows
When a door closes, a much larger and better window opens
We must never give up; never say 'never.'
unleash the entrepreneurial ingenuity of our people
put our money where our mouth is
register_markers: Let me show how this interplay has happened in my life
AND NOW, LET ME TAKE UP BRIEFLY
All caps for emphasis (ALL CAPS opening of paragraphs)
decision_framework_signals: applies 'twin beacons' framework to philanthropic strategy: first project for the talented, second for the poor
frames MSME support as the operational mechanism for the 'prosperity' half of his core philosophy
cross_references: Full speech uploaded in cjpanganiban.com and libpros.com
Court resolution naming him 'The Renaissance Jurist of the 21st Century'
notable_anecdotes: couldn't enter UP because family couldn't afford the 15-centavo Diliman bus fare
Yale full scholarship turned to dust when US Embassy refused him a visa due to student activism
11+ years as virtual hermit jurist producing 1,200 signed decisions and a book a year
Ryan Cayabyab's 'Ageless Passion' musicale inspiring him to operationalize his philosophy
P2M personal seed contribution to FLP, growing to P50M+ for Makati head office
donating his family's P100M+ residential lot in Paseo de Magallanes to seed FLP's ultimate projects
